title: Bee Removal in New Brighton, Staten Island | $350-$550 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional bee removal services in New Brighton, Staten Island. Safe, humane bee extraction for homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call today for a quote!
intro: When bees establish hives in your New Brighton property, swift and professional removal is essential for the safety of your family and neighbors. Best Pest Control NYC provides expert bee removal services throughout New Brighton, from the historic homes near Lafayette Avenue to the residential streets surrounding Clove Lakes Park. Our experienced technicians safely relocate bee colonies while protecting your property from damage.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served Staten Island communities like New Brighton for over a decade, combining local expertise with humane bee removal practices. Our licensed technicians understand the unique challenges of New Brighton's mix of Victorian-era homes and modern residences, ensuring safe removal without harming the beneficial bee population.
localContext: New Brighton's tree-lined streets and proximity to the Staten Island Ferry terminal create ideal conditions for bee activity, especially during warmer months. The neighborhood's historic architecture, including older homes with eaves and attics, often provides attractive nesting sites for honeybees and carpenter bees seeking shelter.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove a bee hive in New Brighton?
faqs.answer: We typically respond to New Brighton bee removal requests within 24 hours. Emergency same-day service is available for dangerous situations involving aggressive swarms or hives near entryways. Our team will assess the location and safely remove the colony as quickly as possible.

faqs.question: What's included in the $350-$550 bee removal price?
faqs.answer: Our bee removal service includes a thorough inspection, safe hive extraction, relocation of the colony when possible, removal of honeycomb and residue, treatment of the affected area to prevent re-infestation, and sealing of entry points. The final cost depends on hive size, location accessibility, and structural complexity.

faqs.question: Do you exterminate bees or relocate them?
faqs.answer: Whenever possible, we humanely relocate bee colonies to appropriate apiaries where they can continue pollinating safely. We only recommend extermination in cases of highly aggressive Africanized bees or when relocation poses significant safety risks. Our priority is preserving these beneficial insects while protecting your property.

faqs.question: Can bees damage my New Brighton home?
faqs.answer: Yes, bee colonies can cause structural damage over time. Honeybees create heavy wax combs that can stain ceilings and walls, while carpenter bees bore tunnels into wood siding, eaves, and decks. The honey attracts other pests, and moisture from the hive can lead to mold growth. Prompt removal prevents costly repairs to your New Brighton property.
